@@ -943,6 +943,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-m5wf.9.4","title":"Add integration tests for under-tested cross-crate interactions","description":"## Purpose

Add integration tests for cross-crate interactions that are currently under-tested.

## Scope

1. **ffs-core ↔ ffs-repair** — End-to-end repair cycle through core orchestration layer. Corrupt → detect → repair → verify.
2. **ffs-mvcc ↔ ffs-journal** — WAL-backed MVCC with journal replay after crash. Verify MVCC state recovers correctly.
3. **ffs-block ↔ ffs-alloc** — Allocation through block cache. Verify allocated blocks are readable via block cache.
4. **ffs-dir ↔ ffs-inode ↔ ffs-extent** — Create file via directory, allocate inode, allocate extents, write data, verify round-trip.

## Acceptance criteria
- 4 integration test suites (one per interaction)
- Each suite has 5+ test cases
- Tests exercise error paths as well as happy paths","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:26:54.753146635Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-13T21:37:45.474901469Z","closed_at":"2026-03-13T21:37:45.473982409Z","close_reason":"Completed: Added cross_crate_integration.rs with 23 tests across 4 suites: mvcc_journal (6 tests: commit ordering, snapshot isolation, FCW conflicts, multi-block atomicity, empty txn, nonexistent reads), block_alloc (6 tests: bitmap round-trip, sequential/fragmented allocation, full bitmap, wraparound, clear/realloc), block_readwrite (6 tests: single/multiple/overwrite, unwritten zeros, metadata, block sizes), extent_mapping (5 tests: single/boundary/hole/empty/full resolve). All pass, clippy clean.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["qa_unit_required","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-m5wf.9.4","depends_on_id":"bd-m5wf.9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:26:54.753146635Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-m5wf.9.5","title":"Verification Gate: test coverage hardening targets met","description":"## Purpose

Verify all test coverage hardening targets are met.

## Acceptance criteria
- ffs-tui has >50 unit tests
- FUSE E2E runner works in CI (with graceful skip)
- 3 new fuzz targets implemented and campaigned
- 4 cross-crate integration test suites passing
- Total workspace test count increased by >100","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:26:55.742235112Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-18T06:45:56.123546721Z","closed_at":"2026-03-18T06:45:56.122742108Z","close_reason":"Verification gate: ffs-tui 55 tests (>50 target). FUSE E2E CI-compatible (50 tests un-ignored, 7 new scenarios, CI job added). 3 new fuzz targets (wal_replay, mvcc_operations, extent_tree). 4 cross-crate integration suites (23 tests). Workspace total 2157 tests. xfstests regression gate operational. All structural targets met.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["qa_e2e_required","qa_unit_required","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-m5wf.9.5","depends_on_id":"bd-m5wf.9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:26:55.742235112Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-m5wf.9.5","depends_on_id":"bd-m5wf.9.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:28:44.367913912Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-m5wf.9.5","depends_on_id":"bd-m5wf.9.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:28:45.533217201Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-m5wf.9.5","depends_on_id":"bd-m5wf.9.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:28:46.850663527Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-m5wf.9.5","depends_on_id":"bd-m5wf.9.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:28:48.044536047Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-m9ei","title":"Conformance: add ext4 inline-data fixture + test","description":"Spec §15.8 says inline data read is in-scope. Add a sparse fixture that encodes EXT4_INLINE_DATA_FL with system.data ibody continuation and a harness test that validates parse + read path. Update fixture checksums + golden manifest.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-13T18:06:50.283275100Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-15T00:28:22.032971839Z","closed_at":"2026-04-15T00:28:22.030753549Z","close_reason":"Added ext4_inode_inline_data.json and ext4_inode_inline_data_with_continuation.json fixtures with conformance tests. Tests verify parsing of inline data in i_block field and system.data xattr continuation.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","ext4"]}
+{"id":"bd-m7sat","title":"Saturate harness metrics atomics","description":"Counters and gauges in crates/ffs-harness/src/metrics.rs use atomic fetch_add, which can wrap through negative values after large increments or adjustments. Custom histogram bounds are accepted unsorted even though bucket lookup uses partition_point and requires sorted bounds. Saturate atomic arithmetic and normalize custom bounds with focused regressions.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"cod-fs","created_at":"2026-04-27T09:14:08Z","created_by":"cod-fs","updated_at":"2026-04-27T09:18:33Z","closed_at":"2026-04-27T09:18:33Z","close_reason":"Saturated harness metric counter, gauge, and histogram atomic additions; normalized custom histogram bounds; added overflow and bound-order regressions. Validation passed: focused ffs-harness metrics tests, fmt, diff-check, UBS touched file, workspace check, and workspace clippy.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-ma4w","title":"Repair: remove hardcoded metadata group 0 default in scrub pipeline","description":"ScrubWithRecovery::new currently hardcodes metadata_groups={0}. For configs where group IDs are non-zero or remapped, default refresh and adaptive policy metadata classification are wrong. Derive default metadata group(s) from configured group layouts and add regression tests.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-02-19T02:51:16.017295660Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-19T02:57:49.962316450Z","closed_at":"2026-02-19T02:57:49.962292956Z","close_reason":"Completed: metadata default now derived from configured groups; regression tests added; rch check/clippy/tests pass","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["correctness","metadata","policy","repair"]}
 {"id":"bd-mbd3t","title":"add mounted-path ext4 user.xattr XATTR_CREATE/XATTR_REPLACE errno coverage","description":"Survey finding: ext4 FUSE xattr E2E coverage in `crates/ffs-harness/tests/fuse_e2e.rs` currently proves set/get/list/remove and missing-attribute behavior, but it does not exercise the mounted-path `XATTR_CREATE` / `XATTR_REPLACE` contract that `ffs-fuse` and `ffs-core` already implement. The exact errno mapping for create-vs-replace semantics is therefore only proven below the operator surface.

Scope:
- Add mounted-path helpers that call `setxattr` with explicit `XATTR_CREATE` / `XATTR_REPLACE` flags from Python or a small Rust probe.
- Prove that creating an existing xattr rejects with `EEXIST` and leaves the original value intact.
- Prove that replacing a missing xattr rejects with the stable missing-xattr errno (`ENODATA`/`ENOATTR`) rather than generic `ENOENT`.
- Keep assertions on file contents and unrelated xattrs so the tests catch accidental mutation.

Acceptance:
- ext4 mounted-path tests cover both `XATTR_CREATE` and `XATTR_REPLACE` failure modes with exact errno assertions.
- The post-failure state of the inode and xattr set is unchanged.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T07:29:00.850487704Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T07:47:01.795607846Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T07:47:01.793191110Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-mbyg","title":"[fuse] Guard should_steal against invalid core id","description":"PerCoreDispatcher::should_steal panics if called with a core id >= num_cores; guard and return false to keep API robust.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-11T18:19:47.402610289Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-11T18:21:47.612032122Z","closed_at":"2026-04-11T18:21:47.611833977Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
